report_name: Cisco Patches Critical Unauthenticated RCE Flaw in Smart Software Manager

vulnerabilities.name: CVE-2026-20160
vulnerabilities.description: A critical vulnerability in Cisco Smart Software Manager On-Prem (SSM On-Prem) due to insufficient access control on a specific API. It allows an unauthenticated, remote attacker to execute arbitrary commands with root privileges by sending a crafted HTTP request.

indicators.name: Suspicious Shell Spawned by Cisco SSM On-Prem Process
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious Shell Spawned by Cisco SSM On-Prem Process
id: 2b9e1a3f-8c7d-4b6a-9f0e-1d2c3b4a5b6c
status: experimental
description: Detects the spawning of a shell process (e.g., sh, bash) by a process associated with Cisco Smart Software Manager On-Prem. This could indicate potential exploitation of CVE-2026-20160.
references:
  - https://sec.cloudapps.cisco.com/security/center/content/CiscoSecurityAdvisory/cisco-sa-ssm-cli-execution-cHUcWuNr
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-02
modified: 2026-04-02
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: linux
detection:
  selection_parent:
    ParentImage|contains:
      - '/opt/cisco/ssm'
      - '/var/opt/cisco/ssm'
  selection_child:
    Image|endswith:
      - '/sh'
      - '/bash'
  condition: selection_parent and selection_child
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate administrative scripts or maintenance tasks executed by the appliance.
indicators.description: Detects when a process related to Cisco SSM On-Prem spawns a shell like 'bash' or 'sh', which could indicate exploitation of CVE-2026-20160.
